observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: MDRRM Council, through the MDRRMO, comment on the observations noted on the budgeting process of the DRRM fund and provide for the lacking budgetary documents, if any, for audit purposes; Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer and other personnel concerned strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 14, 2014, and COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 in the utilization of cash and in-kind donations received by the Municipality; MSWDO, Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned submit the distribution lists of the procured and distributed relief goods during the pandemic crisis or audit purposes; MSWDO submit to the Audit Team the acknowledgement receipts of in-kind donations received and proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; Concerned Municipal Officials (i) provide justification on the charging of ineligible expenses under the BGCM fund, and (ii) comment on the observations noted on the procurement process undertaken by the Municipality during the COVID-19 situation; (iii) submit the lacking documents for audit purposes; and (iv) ensure that future claims are supported with complete supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management (i) provided justificatio n on the charging of incomplete pictures and SAC details, and (ii) will provide the lacking SAC and pictures for audit purposes; (iii) will study the COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 for compliance; and (iv) ensure that future claims are supported with complete supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason: Substantiall y submitted lacking documents except for documents related to procurement

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO and Municipal Accountant to provide justification on the incomplete details supplied in the SAC and grant of cash assistance to ineligible beneficiaries, and submit the lacking SAC and pictures to substantiate the complete liquidation of the fund transfer.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted justification on the incomplete details supplied in the SAC and grant of cash assistance to ineligible beneficiaries, and (ii) requested extension for the submission of justification for inclusion of identified ineligible beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Documents supporting SAC forms of Barangay San Isidro and explanation on inclusion of ineligible beneficia ries on the SAP were still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) record the inventory of the unused and unissued Accountable Forms in the books of accounts in accordance with IPSAS 12, (ii) GSO-designate prepare and maintain stock cards pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, (iii) Municipal Accountant record the inventory of the unused and unissued accountable forms in the books of accounts in accordance with the provisions of Paragraph 11 of IPSAS 12, and (iv) ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording transactions in the books of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management will provide stock room in the proposed municipal building; and will assign staff to perform tasks relative to inventory record keeping.
observations.reason: Lack of funds and technical personnel to maintain systematic inventory of supplies

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: GSO-Designate (i) conduct a complete physical count and technical valuation of the property, plant and equipment to establish existence, ownership and condition of the properties owned by the Municipality particularly on the local road network to determine the current value of the road components and facilitate the preparation of the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and ensure that the required details in the reports were accurately and completely filled out; Municipal Accountant and GSO allot time and effort to (i) coordinate and prepare complete supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts especially the previous year’s PPE acquisitions, (ii) prepare and maintain property records and reports for all PPE accounts especially for the Road Networks, and (iii) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to complete the maintenance of a road map with a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; Appraisal and Disposal Committee account all determined unserviceable properties to facilitate the preparation of required supporting documents for disposal; and Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: Ledger cards were maintained for acquired PPEs from CY 2020; The MEO is currently updating the Road Map with its complete description and cost segregation, and will provide a copy to the Municipal Accountant and GSO upon completion; The GSO is reviewing the list of PPEs of the accounting office to serve as guide in the conduct of Physical count; already acquired new format of reports for PPEs; IIRUP will be accomplished upon completion of physical count to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: Inability to conduct physical count and update of the Road Map due to lack of ample time and limited manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) exert continuous effort to collate the details about the long outstanding Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts; (ii) refund/return the excess amount of funds from the fully completed projects to the source agencies pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and (iii) coordinate with the implementing offices/agencies of the long outstanding balances of fund transfers so that appropriate actions may be undertaken thereon for effective and efficient utilization of the funds.
observations.managementAction: Management (i) sent letters to various funding agencies with long outstanding balance to reconcile records; and (ii) refunded some of the unexpended balance to source agencies.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving files and collating details of long outstanding balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) reconcile with the records of the Audit Team and cause the refund of the excessive payment for travelling expenses amounting to ₱7,350.00 to avoid disallowance in audit; (ii) observe compliance with the pertinent provisions of EO No. 77 and DILG MC No. 2019-82; and (iii) ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the liquidation of cash advances and reimbursements of travelling expenses in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions.
observations.managementAction: Concerned municipal employees submit the lacking documents in the liquidation of cash advances and reimbursements of travelling expenses and allowances in compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Settlement of excess TEV claims is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: BAC (i) provide justification on the noted lapses in the details in the bidding documents, (ii) abide with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 particularly Sections 37.1.6 in the posting of the NOA and 37.4.2 in the posting of NTP, and (iii) ensure validity and propriety of the details in the bidding documents; Municipal Accountant provide explanation on the delayed payments of the suppliers/contractors; and BAC, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and concerned employees submit justification on the noted observations and the lacking documents for audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and Accounting Office provided explanation on the lapses and more careful in checking the documents on the succeeding procurements. Some of the lacking documents were submitted.
observations.reason: Collation/submission of lacking documents is in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: BAC, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant, and the concerned municipal officials and employees submit justification on the non-compliance to the MOA for the Construction of National Child Development Center regarding the delay in its implementation and submit the noted lacking documents for audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and Municipal Accountant provided explanation on the (i) lapses noted in the compliance to the relevant provisions on Warranty Security, Invitation to Observers, execution of performance bond, and inconsistencies in the supporting documents; (ii) absence of Notice to Losing Bidders; and (iii) delayed posting of NOA and NTP. Moreover, ensure that the details in the bidding documents and other supporting documents are properly indicated to verify the validity and propriety of each of the documents; and abide with the Revised IRR.
observations.reason: Collation/submission of lacking documents is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (i) observe strict compliance with the guideline set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 in determining allowable development projects to be funded under the 20% Development Fund, and confirm the feasibility of the particular PPAs and observe timeliness of their implementation to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and Municipal Engineer and the BAC periodically provide the MBO and the MAccO with the status of projects and the procurements undertaken by the Municipality to assist the concerned offices in the periodic review and monitoring of the balances of current and continuing appropriations.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO being the secretariat of LDC maintains good communication and continuous consultation with the members of the LDC and includes all office involve in the implementati on of the projects. The MBO will continuously monitor funds allocated to all development projects.
observations.reason: Implementati on of the projects were hampered by the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. MDRRM Council, through the MDRRMO, comment on the observations
noted on the budgeting process of the DRRM fund and provide for the
lacking budgetary documents, if any, for audit purposes;
b. Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the year-end trial balance and
financial statement for the SAGF-BGCM in compliance with the rules and
regulation set under Section 107 of Chapter 6, Volume I of the Manual on the
NGAS for LGUs for fair presentation, transparency and accountability of the
funds utilized;
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer and other personnel
concerned strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002
dated April 14, 2014, and COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 in
the utilization of cash and in-kind donations received by the Municipality;
d. MSWDO submit to the Audit Team the acknowledgement receipts of in-kind
donations received and proof of receipt by and distribution to the
beneficiaries;
e. MSWDO, Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned submit the
distribution lists of the procured and distributed relief goods during the
pandemic crisis or audit purposes; and
f. Concerned Municipal Officials (i) provide justification on the charging of
ineligible expenses under the BGCM fund; (ii) comment on the observations
noted on the procurement process undertaken by the Municipality during
the COVID-19 situation; (iii) submit the lacking documents for audit
purposes; and (iv) ensure that future claims are supported with complete
supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree
No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO and
Municipal Accountant to provide justification on the incomplete details supplied in
the SAC and grant of cash assistance to ineligible beneficiaries, and submit the
lacking SAC and pictures to substantiate the complete liquidation of the fund
transfer.

We recommended that the:
a. Supply Officer/GSO-designate conduct physical count of Inventories and
prepare the RPCI per account in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual
on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and conduct regular reconciliation
between the Accounting and GSO records for inventory accounts and effect
the necessary adjustments, if warranted;
b. GSO-designate prepare and maintain stock cards pursuant to Section 114 of
the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
c. Municipal Accountant record the inventory of the unused and unissued
accountable forms in the hands of the custodian of Accountable Forms, and
the balance of inventories at the end of the year in the books of accounts in
accordance with the provisions of Paragraph 11 of IPSAS 12. Henceforth,
ensure that procurement of supplies and materials be recorded in the
appropriate asset account and appropriate expense account pursuant to the
asset method of accounting prescribed under Section 114 of the Manual for
NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the:
a. GSO-Designate (i) conduct a complete physical count and technical valuation
of the property, plant and equipment to establish existence, ownership and
condition of the properties owned by the Municipality particularly on the
local road network to determine the current value of the road components
and facilitate the preparation of the RPCLRN; (ii) ensure that the required
details in the reports are accurately and completely filled up; (iii) analyze the
items included in the RPCPPE for proper classification; (iv) prepare and
maintain RLRN and Road Map to account for the Local Roads and reconcile
with the Registry of Public Infrastructures maintained by the Accounting
Office; and (v) collate the documents of the land owned by the Municipal
Government that remained uncovered by a Transfer Certificate of Title
(TCT);
b. Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate allot time and effort to (i)
coordinate and prepare complete supporting schedules to facilitate the
reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence,
ownership and condition of the properties; (ii) prepare and maintain
75
appropriate and required property records and reports for all PPE accounts
especially on the Road Networks; and (iii) coordinate with the Municipal
Engineer to complete the maintenance of a road map with complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
c. Appraisal and Disposal Committee account all determined unserviceable
properties to facilitate the preparation of required supporting documents for
disposal; and
d. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with
complete description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) exert continous effort
to collate the details about the long outstanding Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs
accounts; (ii) return the excess amount of funds from the fully completed
projects to the source agencies pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No.
94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and (iii) coordinate with the implementing
offices/agencies of the long outstanding balances of fund transfers so that
appropriate actions could be undertaken thereon for effective and efficient
utilization of the fund and ensure that the intended beneficiaries could timely
benefit therefrom.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) reconcile with the records of the Audit Team and
cause the refund of the excessive payment for travelling expenses amounting
to ₱7,350.00 to avoid disallowance in audit; (ii) observe compliance with the
pertinent provisions of EO No. 77 dated March 15, 2019 in the approval of
subsequent claims for traveling expenses; and (iii) ensure that complete
supporting documents are attached to the liquidations of cash advances and
reimbursements for travelling expenses; and
b. Concerned municipal employees submit the lacking documents in the
liquidation of cash advances and reimbursements of traveling expenses and
allowances in compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC (i) provide justification on the noted lapses in the details in the bidding
documents, (ii) abide with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184
particularly Sections 37.1.6 in the posting of the NOA and 37.4.2 in the
posting of NTP, and (iii) ensure validity and propriety of the details in the
bidding documents;
b. Municipal Accountant provide explanation on the delayed payments of the
suppliers/contractors; and
c. BAC, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant, and concerned employees
submit justification on the noted observations and the lacking documents for
audit purposes.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. BAC and Municipal Accountant provide explanation on the (i) lapses noted
in the compliance to the relevant provisions on Warranty Security,
Invitation to Observers, execution of performance bond, and inconsistencies
in the supporting documents; (ii) absence of Notice to Losing Bidders; and
(iii) delayed posting of NOA and NTP PhilGEPS website. Moreover ensure
that the details in the bidding documents and other supporting documents
are properly indicated to verify the validity and propriety of each of the
97
documents; and abide with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184
particularly on the relevant provisions on warranty, invitation to observes,
and posting of the NOA and NTP; and
b. BAC, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant, and the concerned
municipal officials and employees submit justification on the non-compliance
to the MOA for the Construction of National Child Development Center
regarding the delay in its implementation and submit the noted lacking
documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the:
a. Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning
Development Coordinator observe strict compliance with the guideline set
forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 in determining allowable development
projects to be funded under the 20% Development Fund, and confirm the
feasibility of the particular PPAs and observe timeliness of their
implementation to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes; and
b. Municipal Engineer and the BAC periodically provide the MBO and the
MAccO of the status of projects and the procurements undertaken by the
Municipality to assist the concerned offices in the periodic review and
monitoring of the balances of current and continuing appropriations, and
immediately inform the MBO and MAccO whenever there are changes on
the awarded PPAs and problems in the project’s implementation to make the
necessary adjustment in the financial reports relative to the affected PPAs.

We recommended that the:
a. MDRRM Officer (i) monitor and facilitate the implementation of the PPAs
under the LDRRMF by monitoring the period of its implementation and
ensure that all projects appropriated in LDRRMF be implemented during
the year; (ii) provide explanation on the non-preparation of LDRRMFIP for
the current appropriation for CY 2020 and revised/supplemental
LDRRMFIP for the reprogrammed PPAs in current and continuing
appropriations. Henceforth, ensure that PPAs of LDRRMF be included in
LDRRMFIP and integrated in AIP;
b. MDRRMO, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer ensure the
correctness of the amounts in the reports maintained by them;
c. MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer evaluate the PPAs in the
continuing appropriation and make appropriate actions thereto by
reprogramming to another PPAs to help in reducing exposure to potential
impact of hazards and related disaster in the Municipality; and
d. Municipal Accountant review the entries made relating to the DRRM funds
received from other sources and effect necessary adjusting entries, if
warranted and be guided by COA Circular No. 2012-002 in ensuring that the
DRRM funds to be received by the Municipality from other sources be
treated under special trust liability account.

We recommended that the:
a. MSWMB (i) fast track the preparation of the SCRP and 10-Year SWMP for
approval of the NSWMC; (ii) enforce close supervision with the BSWMC to
ensure that the same are all active and functional; (iii) promote continuous
segregation of waste at source through IEC drives and assess regularly the
funding and resource requirements of the SWM plan to ensure
implementation and realization of all SWM programs and projects; and (iv)
through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of
barangay officials to support the SWM program of the government by
educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable
or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and maintenance of
materials recovery facilities; and
b. Local Finance Committee, through the Municipal Budget Officer,
appropriate funds for SWM projects geared towards the realization of the
goals of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of SLF.

We recommended that the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer institute appropriate action against the
delinquent market stallholders and water consumers for collection of their
unpaid bills and ensure strict compliance to the pertinent provisions of the
2004 Revenue Code of the Municipality relative the delinquencies in market
stallholders and water concessionaires; and
b. Municipal Accountant review the entries made under Accounts Receivable
and effect necessary adjustment, if warranted, and adhere to Annex B of
COA Circular No. 2015-009, Revised Chart of Accounts for Local
Government Units in the succeeding transactions.

We recommended that the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer intensify tax collection efficiency by (a)
continuous sending of the notices of delinquency to delinquent taxpayers to
collect and realize the tax collectibles, and eliminate the delinquencies or be
kept to a minimal amount; (b) strictly enforce the pertinent provisions of RA
7160 relative to the actions on delinquent real properties; and (c)
design/adopt measures and strategies to remedy the low collection of current
year’s tax collectibles and delinquencies; and
b. Municipal Assessor devise an effective way to address the problems
encountered during tax mapping and assessment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person
and GAD key personnel to:
a. ensure that identified gender issue as well as its corresponding objectives are
specific, measurable, attainable, realistic and time-bound so that
accomplishment of the same could be reliably measured. Moreover, see to it
that GAD objectives, activities, targets and performance indicators are
enhanced to better articulate the responses to the identified gender issues
and/or its causes;
b. ensure the submission of GPB on or before March 31 to the DILG Provincial
Office pursuant to Section 4.0 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-
01, amending Section C.5 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
c. submit to the Audit Team the DILG-received GAD AR and its supporting
documents for CY 2020 and ensure timely preparation submission of the said
report in the succeeding years pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001;
d. facilitate the preparation of the GAD agenda as well as continuous updating
of data for the GAD database pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC
No. 2016-01, amending PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as basis
in the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR; and
e. ensure and monitor attendance of the GPFS members to GAD required
trainings to capacitate and enhance their knowledge and build their
competencies on GAD related laws and GAD-related tools, among others.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to enforce the
settlement of the issued suspensions and disallowances and take appropriate action
against the persons deemed responsible and liable thereof pursuant to the above
cited provisions of the RRSA.

We recommended that the: OIC-Municipal Accountant record the supplies and materials to Inventory account upon purchase and Expense account upon issuance thereof pursuant to the asset method of accounting for recording purchases of supplies and materials as prescribed under Section 114 of the Manual for NGAS-LGU. Likewise, the OIC-Municipal Treasurer maintain supplies ledger card and supplies stock card, respectively, for each stock of inventory to properly account the issuance of its inventories to avoid possible misuse and wastage. Henceforth, conduct physical count of inventories and prepare Report of Physical Inventories, and furnish the COA Auditor of such report.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: No designated supply officer to handle the task and the LGU didn’t have stock room, hence office supplies were being issued immediately to respective offices and recorded the same as expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer reconcile their cash records and settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts per GL and cashbooks; (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the identified negative balance in the cash in bank, and (iii) review the long outstanding/stale checks and require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Accountant with the original DVs and its supporting documents for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed under the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs to ensure recording of such cancellation.
observations.managementAction: Update of records/review of difference, BRS for 1st quarter was prepared.
observations.reason: Reconciliation between Accounting and Treasury Offices was not made due to voluminous workload of both offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant (i) cause the refund of the excessive claims for travelling expenses totaling P12,350.00 from the concerned municipal officials and employees, (ii) ensure strict compliance with the provisions of Section 4 of EO No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 in preparing the claim for travelling expenses, and (iii) avoid allowing tampered official receipts in any government transactions; and (b) OIC-Municipal Engineer and Municipal Civil Registrar explain the reason why the Pipefitter Foreman detailed under the Municipal Engineering Office was allowed to attend the convention intended for the Municipal Civil Registrar and its staff.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued to concerned officials and employees; The HRMO and MCR refunded the excess travel claims; Reminded the management to comply with the travel law during ManCom Meeting; Prohibited tampering of documents attached in the vouchers.
observations.reason: Lack of personal funds of the officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer coordinate with each other and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the proper reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties; Municipal Accountant analyze the nature of transactions and make an adjustment to properly classify the items to a more appropriate account; ICO-Municipal Treasurer (i) prepare and maintain appropriate property records and reports for PPE account, (ii) account all determined unserviceable properties to facilitate the preparation of IIRUP and other required supporting documents for disposal, and (iii) prepare and maintain RLRN and Road Map to account for the Local Roads and reconcile with the Registry of Public Infrastructures maintained by the Accounting Office; Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count and technical valuation of the local road network to determine the current value of the road components to facilitate the preparation of the RPCLRN; and Municipal Engineer maintain road map and provide the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The MaccO provided the MTO list of PPE per office to verify the PAR No. and accountable person; provided the MTO with proper format of property record and reports; reviewd the PAR records. Provided each office of the list for verification of the properties’ condition. Reviewed the list provided by the COA to effect the adjustments.
observations.reason: Voluminous workload and lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: BAC Chairman provide acceptable justification on (i) contracting with suppliers/contractors with doubtful qualifications, (ii) untimely execution of performance security and notice to proceed, and (iii) all other observations regarding the procurement of the projects; Municipal Accountant (i) provide acceptable explanation on the incurrence of overdraft for the Construction of Evacuation Center at Barangay Maasin; (ii) adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 regarding the proper classification of accounts and reclassify the recording of the three projects to their appropriate asset and expense account; and (iii) submit the notarized Memorandum of Agreement entered into with the Department of Health, and all the lacking documents for audit purposes; ICO-Municipal Treasurer in her capacity as General Services Officer of the Municipal Government make appropriate action in order that the procured three units dump truck be in compliant with the said Memorandum Circular; and OIC- Municipal Engineer provide explanation on the deviation of the implementation mode from by contract to by administration for the Construction of Evacuation Center at Barangay Maasin and Milagrosa.
observations.managementAction: Submitted justificatio n for the observations noted. The MAccO provided MEO a copy of Program of Works and audit results. Follow up funds to the DILG for the evacuation center/immediately submitted reports to COA and DILG. Vehicles were marked with “For Official Use Only”; The OIC – MT instructed the concerned personnel to remove the signage of the vehicle which was not in accordance with DILG Memo Circular. The MEO will submit written explanation.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with heavy responsibilities and sufficient time to comply with Audit Team’s recommendation on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman (i) ensure that the bidding documents were properly dated in order to verify the validity and propriety of each of the documents submitted by the prospective bidder; (ii) observe the timeline in the execution of the contract agreement and other pertinent bidding document in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (iii) ensure that the Notice of Award/Letter of Award is duly received by the supplier/winning bidder in order to be certain on the date of entering into contract; (iv) post the NOA and NTP in PhilGEPS website, in agency’s website, and three conspicuous places as required under Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and (v) submit justification on the noted observations on the procurement of the projects.; Municipal Accountant provide explanation on the delayed payment of the Construction of Sitio Lagnas, Barangay San Juan, Barangay Poblacion Water System Phase 1; and Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairperson and Municipal Engineer submit all the lacking documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Observed procurement procedure and properly prepared bidding documents. Reviewed and corrected the signatories in the Certificate of Acceptance.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with heavy responsibilities thus pressure of equally important matters and sufficient time to comply with Audit Team’s recommendati on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Mayor: through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the Municipality by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; take appropriate action to fully utilize the MRF located in Brgy Poblacion and provide the necessary support in enjoining the remaining 13 barangays in establishing their own MRF, and (iii) establish MRF in the dumpsite area; recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of RA No. 9003; and see to it that the operation of the open dumpsite is properly controlled and in compliance with Rule XIII- Operation of Controlled Dumpsite of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Submitted justificatio ns on the observations noted. The MAccO provided MEO a copy of Program of Works and audit results. Follow up funds to the DILG for the evacuation center/immediately submitted reports to COA and DILG. Vehicles were marked with “For Official Use Only”; The OIC – MT instructed the concerned personnel to remove the signage of the vehicle which was not in accordance with DILG Memo Circular. The MEO will submit written explanation.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with heavy responsibilities thus pressure of equally important matters and sufficient time to comply with Audit Team’s recommendation on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Local School Board prepare the PPMP for all the procurement to be undertaken in the SEF and submit the same to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation to an indicative APP; (b) BAC Chairman ensure that all procurement in SEF are in accordance with the approved APP and consider the procurement of common-use supplies and materials in bulk purchases to avail of volume discount and lesser paper work and travelling cost; (c) BAC Secretariat indicate the date and place of delivery, delivery and payment terms in the PO, and ensure that date of receipt of the supplier on the terms of the PO was indicated; and (d) Municipal Accountant submit, for audit purposes, the drivers trip tickets, monthly report of fuel consumption of government motor transportation, and monthly report of official travels pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the consolidated procurement for SEF. Oriented BAC Secretariat on proper preparation of procurement documents. To submit the needed documents to COA.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with heavy responsibilities thus pressure of equally important matters and sufficient time to comply with Audit Team’s recommendation on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: MPDC, MDRRMC and MDRRM Officer provide (i) specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund to easily quantify the attainment of the planned PPAs; (ii) specific period of implementation of the PPAs of MDRRM; and (iii) acceptable justification on the non-preparation of LDRRMFIP for the current appropriation for CY 2019. Further, ensure that PPAs of LDRRMF be included in LDRRMFIP and integrated in AIP; Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant refrain from transferring unexpended balance from completed Capital Outlay projects to special trust fund; MDRRM Officer evaluate the PPAs in the continuing appropriation and special trust fund and make appropriate actions thereto by reprogramming to another MDRRM PPAs to help in reducing exposure to potential impact of hazards and related disasters in the Municipality; and MDRRMO, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson fast-track the implementation of the PPAs under the MDRRM Fund by monitoring the period for which the PPAs will be implemented. Henceforth, ensure that all projects appropriated in MDRRM Fund be implemented during the year it was appropriated in order to increase the capacity of the Municipality to cope with potential hazards and disasters.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the APP Consolidated the procurement for SEF. Oriented BAC Secretariat on proper preparation of procurement documents. To submit the needed documents to COA. Set special meeting and assign responsible person for every program to ensure full implementation on of program and utilization of fund. Partially reviewed the project implementation on; Project implementation is on schedule. Program of Works being prepared. Processing of Purchase Request ahead of time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) MPDC and MDRRMO provide (i) specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund to easily quantify the attainment of the planned PPAs; and (ii) specific period of implementation of the PPAs of MDRRM; (b) Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant regularly reconcile their records to avoid inappropriate charging of PPAs in trust fund to general fund; (c) MDRRM Officer evaluate the PPAs in the continuing appropriation and special trust fund and make appropriate actions thereto by reprogramming to another MDRRM PPAs to help in reducing exposure to potential impact of hazards and related disasters in the Municipality; and (d) MDRRMO, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson fast-track the implementation of the PPAs under the MDRRM Fund by monitoring the period for which the PPAs will be implemented.

observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower and voluminous workload, implementation on of the 20% DF PPAs could not be facilitated. Likewise, due to lack of proper planning inclusion of lump-sum appropriation in 20% DF could not be avoided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: (a) ensure that collections of the collectors are within the maximum cash accountability; (b) refrain from issuing accountable forms to non-bonded employees pursuant to Section 101 of PD 1445, Section 305(f) of RA 7160, Section 50, Chapter 9, Subtitle B, Book V, EO No 292 and Bureau of Treasury, Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 5, 2009; and (iii) review and verify the reconciling items identified in the BRS and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank account; (c) discontinue the practice of keeping large amount of collections in their hand/possession to avoid possible loss and misappropriation. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the transfer of unutilized balance of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 5.1.13 and 5.1.14 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 regarding the proper recognition of expenditures and the proper transfer to General Fund book the unutilized balance of DRRM Fund. We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the transfer of unutilized balance of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant prepared the Journal Entry Voucher to effect the transfer of unutilized balance of LDRRMF. Unutilized fund balances were transferred and appropriate adjustments were effected.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel and voluminous work to collate the documents needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. The MDRRMO to explain why the fund for the purchase of the service/rescue vehicle was sourced from the MDRRMF – Trust Fund instead of the continuing appropriation as stated in the authority to purchase given by DILG; b. The Municipal Treasurer to explain why payment was made before actual delivery and acceptance of the service vehicle; c. The HRMO to explain why the scope and nature of work of the job-order workers in the construction of operation center were not included in the plantilla of contractual appointment; d. The Municipal Accountant explain why no retention was made on the payment of materials in the construction of operation center in consonance with the provision of Section 62.1 of IRR of RA 9184; e. The BAC Chairman and MDRRMO to explain the noted observations in the procurement of the service vehicle, motorized banca, and construction of operation center; and f. The OIC-Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman to submit all the lacking documents pertaining the procurement of service vehicle, motorized banca, and materials and labor for the construction of operation center to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The concerned municipal officials were instructed to comply with the recommendations. Some clarifications and supporting documents were provided and coordinated.
observations.reason: Limited staff, collating of lacking documents are still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Implementing Office to submit the documents needed to validate the compliance of the Municipality with the provisions of the Department of the Interior and Local Government Memorandum Circular No. 2015-74 dated July 14, 2015; The BAC Chairman to explain why the contract cost exceeds the fund allocated by DILG. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to explain why payment exceeds the available cash and to what fund does the excess payment came from; (c) The Municipal Engineer to explain why the contractor took 126 calendar days to complete the project; (d) The OIC – Municipal Accountant to submit the paid disbursement voucher for the claim of the contractor of the 15% mobilization amounting to P1,729,536.70; and (e) The OIC-Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairman and Municipal Engineer to submit all the lacking documents noted in audit to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Implementing Office submitted the documents needed to validate compliance. Reviewed CY 2015 Registry for 20% Development Fund Projects. An obligation amounting to P76,409.18 was recorded to transfer fund from 20% DF to Trust Fund as counterpart for DILG Salintubig Project. The BAC Chairman to explain why the contract cost exceeds the fund allocated by DILG.
observations.reason: Limited staff, collating of lacking documents are still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Implementing Office to submit the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the Municipality and Department of the Interior and Local Government for the implementation of Umabang and Bailan Water System Level II, and Rehabilitation/Expansion of Tambangan Water System Projects. Likewise, MOA between DSWD and the Municipality for the implementation of the Rehabilitation/ re-gravelling of Milagrosa to Bating and Cabugao Farm-to-Market Roads and Concreting of Bagong Sikat to Talon. The OIC – Municipal Accountant and ICO – Municipal Treasurer explain why payment for 2nd progress billing for the DILG project exceeds the cash available; The BAC Chairman and OIC-Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking documents noted in audit to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Implementing Office submitted the MOA and lacking documents. The concerned municipal officials were instructed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel and voluminous workload to do the task.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, BAC Chairman and OIC-Municipal Accountant to explain non-compliance with the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); provide acceptable justification on the non-compliance of the Municipality with the provisions of the MOA; and submit the paid disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Some of the lacking documents were already submitted. The unexpended balance was returned to DILG.
observations.reason: Some of the lacking documents were already submitted. The unexpended balance was returned to DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: (a) The Implementing Office to submit the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the Municipality and Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) for the Improvement of Benli FMR Phase IV. Likewise, explain non-compliance with the provisions of the MOA and DILG-OPAPP JMC No. 2 series of 2014 dated June 9, 2014; (b) The OIC – Municipal Accountant and ICO – Municipal Treasurer return the unexpended balance of the fund to DILG since the project was already completed and all payments were already made; (c) The BAC Chairman and OIC-Municipal Accountant to explain the above noted observations regarding the procurement and implementation of the projects; and (d) The OIC-Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairman and Municipal Engineer to submit all the lacking documents noted in audit to avoid suspension in audit; (e) The Municipal Mayor require the contractor to correct the deficiencies noted, otherwise, demand the payment equivalent to P877,317.96; (f) The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare required JEVs; etc.
observations.managementAction: MOA and lacking documents were partially submitted. Unexpended balance was returned to DILG since the project was completed and all payments were already made. The contractor was demanded to correct the deficiencies.
observations.reason: Management has not yet sent letter to the contractor for the correction of deficiencies. Due to lack of available personnel, collating of other lacking documents were still not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with Section 7 of PD No. 198.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Provide justification on the hiring of 253 job order personnel;
Ensure that hiring of JO personnel is on account of necessity and urgency of the agency; Limit the hiring of job order personnel to complement only the office where transactions are voluminous;
Ensure that the functions and responsibilities of the contracted employees must be well-defined and clear in the contracts/appointments to facilitate proper evaluation of work performed by the employees; and
Formulate hiring plan of job orders to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto; and
Municipal Accountant:
Ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents to establish the validity of claims for payment; and
Remind the concerned JOs to prepare/submit the Daily Time Records and Accomplishment Reports.

We recommended that:
The Municipal officials convene the GAD focal person and require them to develop performance or success indicators for each GAD activity and evaluate the priority projects for GAD during the year; and
The OIC-MSWDO provide explanation on the cost variances of GAD and submit on a timely basis the GAD Plan and Budget and Annual Accomplishment Report as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2004.
observations.reason: No comment/ justificatio n was given by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor secure the proper authority to purchase from the Secretary of the DILG in the acquisition of one (1) unit of Toyota Hilux.
The Municipality ensure compliance with Executive Order No. 292 and relevant administrative orders.
observations.managementAction: Not acted upon by the Management.
observations.reason: The Management is still in process of collating the required documents.
No comments/ justificatio n were submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to include in the monthly report of sources and utilization of LDRRMF the amount of P2,545,446.20 as available source for Continuing Appropriation and the corresponding disbursements of P2,390,026.20 as various disbursements charged to the Continuing Appropriation for LDRRMF.
The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to classify and identify the specific expense account to be used under various programs and activities of LDRRMF to ensure proper charging of expenses to the available appropriation; 
The LDRRMO to avoid using LDRRM fund for non-disaster related activities; and
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers are supported with the necessary documentary requirements before its approval for payment of claims against government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer committed to comply with the recommendations and classify and identify the specific expense account to be used under various programs and activities of LDRRMF. The LDRRMO committed to avoid using LDRRMF for non-disaster related activities.
observations.reason: The concerned employees are loaded with heavy responsibilities thus pressure of equally important matters and sufficient time to update the records.

observations.reason: The Management is still in the process if preparing the internal guidelines for approval of the Mayor of the specific criteria, administrative procedures and standards on the grant of specific Magna Carta benefits to all entitled Public Health Workers.

observations.recommendation:

The MTO conduct of the physical inventory by initially considering the Motor Vehicle, Computers or per classification of PPE in the financial statements, tag those counted property, update the PC, reconcile with accounting record and prepare partial RPCPPE;
Maintain the PPELC required in Section 13 of MNGAS, Volume II, which reconcile with the General Ledger (GL) controlling account; and
The MTO and AU reconcile the Inventory Report and the GL controlling account and prepare adjustment to affected records.

observations.recommendation:

Prepare the BRS every end of each month for all bank accounts and submits the same within the 10 days from the receipt of BS; and
Update the records with LBP – Roxas (PGBS Fund) which pertains to dormant accounts to avoid bank charges in the future.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant submitted Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2015 and 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Government Officials and member of the Local Development Council evaluate the viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional funds needed to complete a project programmed in the previous years; and
The Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of those projects in order to ensure their completion within the target date in order to preclude the unwarranted delay of the project completion, anxiety of enjoying the benefits therefrom by the intended beneficiaries; and
Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of R.A. 7160; and
Municipal Engineer periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations, and submit a list of all completed projects to support the reversion of such unexpended balance to the unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to obligate P2,694,033.16 in CY 2016 from the continuing appropriation.
observations.reason: Some projects were discontinued by the current administration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Government Officials and member of the Local Development Council ensure that the 20% DF is appropriated and disbursed only for its intended purpose in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
Furthermore, the Municipal officials concerned must ensure that the 20% DF is appropriated and disbursed only for its intended purpose in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review and revise the appropriations as necessary.
observations.managementAction: Projects were renamed by the Municipality to conform to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Consider revisiting the pro-forma Market Lease Contracts that the MGOB is using and effect necessary revisions, if any;
Strictly enforce the pertinent provisions of the New Revenue Code and likewise incorporate the same in the Lease Contracts relative to the automatic revocation of cancellation of the contract upon non-compliance with the terms and conditions as agreed upon and the present occupants be given preference over the stalls upon payment of required fees; and
Properly monitor all the stall market contracts to ensure that only Stallholders with valid contracts occupy the market stalls. Otherwise, impose necessary actions as provided for in the Municipality’s Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: Lease contracts were executed during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Officials consider the options in Section 3.4 of the Local Budget Circular No. 100 wherein the personnel in the Operation of Public Cemetery be transferred to the Plantilla of Personnel under the General Fund due to continuous incurrence of losses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to increase its collection for CY 2016 to P105,006.17.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
MDRRMO prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before 15th day after the end of each month; MDRRMO ensure that all the proposed programs/projects for disaster be implemented during the year to ascertain the readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity; and in case there is change in the previously proposed projects, see to it that the disaster council is guided by DILG, DBM and National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council Joint Circular No.2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 wherein the unexpended balance of disaster fund may be released and utilized for new expenditure items upon submission of a revised annual Investment Plan (AIP) as approved by the local Sanggunian and the LCE upon recommendation the council and in accordance with the approved disaster plan; MDRRMO avoid using LDRRMF for travelling allowances of Job Order personnel of the MGOB; and Municipal Accountant ensures that disbursement vouchers are supported with the necessary documentary requirements before its approval for payment of claims against government funds.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer has reconciled the balances that appeared in the SAAOB and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF. The monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF were submitted. Moreover, the MDRRM Office has already stop using LDRRMF for travelling expenses.
observations.reason: Non-monitori ng of funds

observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required reports. Municipal Engineer prepared the monthly monitoring report of the awarded infra PPAs. Designated GSO together with concerned Department Heads conducted semestral physical count. Municipal Accountant made representation with depository banks; and OIC-Municipal Treasurer ensured timely transmittal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal General Services Officer see to it that all government motor vehicles be marked “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” pursuant to COA Circular No. 75-6 dated November 6, 1975.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality marked its vehicle with “For Official Use Only”.

The ICO-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Treasurer acting as General Service Officer (GSO) minimized the delay in remittance and committed to ensuring bonded employees assigned in collecting function. Also, representations were made with banks for reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the and the MDRRMO agreed to prepare and submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan in accordance with the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to reflect the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded, as well as the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality prepared and submitted LDRRMFIP about the current appropriation without taking into consideration the proposed projects and programs to be funded out of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF.
